project: Adult Player Lifecycle — Phase 5: Full Player Portal Remaining Sections
description: Completes the remaining player portal sub-pages: My Progress (read-only sport passport ratings with trend arrows), My Passport Sharing (player takes over sharing controls from guardian on account claim), My Injuries (view injury history and self-report new injuries), and Coach Feedback & AI Summaries (player view of approved coachParentSummaries publicSummary records). All leverage existing backend queries and UI patterns.
readinessLevel: 100% - Existing passport, sharing, injury, and coachParentSummaries backend confirmed in codebase

criticalContext: Phase 1 (portal layout + sidebar) must be complete before this phase — these pages slot into the sidebar navigation created in Phase 1
EXTEND existing backend only — sportPassports, passportSharing, and playerInjuries backend already exists
Sport passport queries: find getPassportsForPlayer or equivalent in packages/backend/convex/models/sportPassports.ts — read before implementing
Passport sharing: existing parent sharing UI at apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/parents/sharing/page.tsx is the template to mirror exactly — read it first
passportSharing table and backend queries already exist for parent-controlled sharing — reuse them for player-controlled sharing

Ownership transfer: when player claims account (Phase 2 claimPlayerAccount), sharing control must transfer from guardian userId to player userId — add this transfer to the claim flow or as a post-claim mutation
Player injuries: find getInjuriesForPlayer or equivalent in packages/backend/convex/models/playerInjuries.ts — read the existing coach injury view for patterns
reportedByRole field: check packages/backend/convex/schema.ts to confirm playerInjuries has reportedByRole field — if missing, add it
Player-reported injuries must appear in coach injury view with 'Player-reported' badge
BetterAuth tables use ctx.runQuery(components.betterAuth.adapter.*). Application tables use ctx.db.
Always use .withIndex() — NEVER .filter(). All queries/mutations must include returns validator.
keyFiles: apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/player/layout.tsx — must exist from Phase 1 before these pages work
apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/parents/sharing/page.tsx — template to mirror for sharing page
packages/backend/convex/schema.ts — confirm reportedByRole on playerInjuries, passportSharing table structure
apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/coach/injuries/ — existing injury page patterns to reference
packages/backend/convex/models/coachParentSummaries.ts — existing coachParentSummaries queries to use for coach feedback page. READ FULLY before implementing US-P5-004
apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/parents/ — find how parents view coachParentSummaries for the UI pattern to mirror

criticalPatterns.ReadOnlyPassport: Player views own passport but cannot edit skill ratings. Remove all edit controls from the coach view pattern. Player CAN add their own notes.
criticalPatterns.SharingTransfer: On claimPlayerAccount, update passportSharing records for this playerIdentityId to transfer guardianId/userId to the player's userId. Guardian sharing access is revoked.
criticalPatterns.SelfReportedInjury: Creates injury with reportedByRole: 'player'. Must appear in coach injury view with 'Player-reported' badge. Verify the field exists in schema before creating.
criticalPatterns.CoachFeedbackVisibility: The coachParentSummaries table has TWO distinct objects: privateInsight (coach raw note — NEVER exposed to players or parents) and publicSummary (AI-generated, shared after coach approval). Adult players see publicSummary records where status is approved/auto_approved/delivered/viewed. The backend query MUST NOT return privateInsight fields. All three sensitivityCategories (normal/injury/behavior) are visible to the player — it is their own data.
criticalPatterns.ReturnValidators: All new queries and mutations must include returns validator.
criticalPatterns.MobileFirst: All pages mobile-first at 375px minimum width.

userStories.title: My Progress — Player View of Sport Passports
userStories.description: As an adult player, I want to see my sport passport ratings and assessment history, so that I can track my own development.
userStories.phase: Phase 5: Full Player Portal Remaining Sections
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/player/progress/page.tsx
Fetch own passports using the appropriate existing query (find in sportPassports.ts — likely getPassportsForPlayer or similar scoped to a playerIdentityId)
If multiple sports: tab or pill switcher at top to select between sports
Display skill ratings (technical, tactical, physical, mental) in read-only format — same visual as coach view but with NO edit controls
Show trend arrows (↑ ↓ =) next to each rating based on most recent vs previous assessment value
Assessment history timeline below ratings: list of assessment dates with summary score for each, newest first
Player CANNOT edit their own ratings — no edit buttons or inputs. Show label: 'Ratings are set by your coach'
Player CAN add their own notes: 'My Notes' textarea section that saves via existing player notes mutation on the passport record — find the existing player notes pattern
Empty state: 'No passport assessments yet. Your coach will complete your first assessment.' if no passports exist
Mobile responsive at 375px minimum
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: Read sportPassports.ts fully before implementing. Mirror the coach passport view but strip all edit capabilities. Find the player notes mutation — it likely already exists.

userStories.title: My Passport Sharing Management (Player Controls Own Sharing)
userStories.description: As an adult player, I want to control who can see my sports passport, so that I can share my data with other clubs when I choose.
userStories.phase: Phase 5: Full Player Portal Remaining Sections
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/player/sharing/page.tsx
Mirror the existing parent sharing UI at apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/parents/sharing/page.tsx — same controls, same patterns, same backend queries
Player can enable/disable passport sharing via toggle — same backend mutations as parent sharing
Player can approve or deny sharing enquiries from other orgs

Sharing ownership transfer: when claimPlayerAccount succeeds (Phase 2, US-P2-003), update passportSharing records for this playerIdentityId to transfer ownership to the player's userId, revoking guardian access
If ownership transfer is not already in Phase 2: add a mutation transferPassportSharingOwnership(playerIdentityId, newUserId) and call it as part of the claim flow or as a post-claim step
If guardian had previously enabled sharing: player sees it is currently shared and can choose to disable
Empty state: 'No sharing requests yet. Enable sharing to let other clubs view your passport.'
Mobile responsive at 375px minimum
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: Read apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/parents/sharing/page.tsx FULLY before implementing. Read the passportSharing table schema. The ownership transfer is the new part — the rest reuses existing backend.

userStories.title: My Injuries — Player Self-View and Self-Report
userStories.description: As an adult player, I want to view and self-report my own injuries, so that I can track my recovery and keep my club informed.
userStories.phase: Phase 5: Full Player Portal Remaining Sections
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/player/injuries/page.tsx
Fetch own injury history using existing injury query scoped to this playerIdentityId (find in playerInjuries.ts)
Show injury list: body part, injury type, severity, date occurred, current status (active/recovering/cleared/healed)
Coach-reported injuries: display read-only with 'Reported by coach' badge
Player-reported injuries: display with 'You reported this' badge
Active injury (if any): show prominently at top with status, expected return date, recovery milestones if available

'Report New Injury' button opens a dialog form with fields: body part (dropdown using existing body part options), injury type (dropdown or text input), severity (minor/moderate/severe radio), date occurred (date picker), occurred during (training/match/other radio), notes (optional textarea)
On submit: create injury record with reportedByRole set to 'player' — verify this field exists in playerInjuries schema; if not, add it to schema.ts first
Player-reported injury appears in coach injury view for that player with 'Player-reported' badge — verify coach view shows reportedByRole
Empty state: 'No injuries on record. Stay healthy!' when no injuries exist
Mobile responsive at 375px minimum
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: Read packages/backend/convex/models/playerInjuries.ts and schema.ts before implementing. Check if reportedByRole exists on playerInjuries — add to schema if missing. Reference apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/coach/injuries/ for existing patterns.

userStories.title: My Coach Feedback & AI Summaries (Player View)
userStories.description: As an adult player, I want to see the feedback and AI summaries my coaches have chosen to share with me so that I can understand my development from my coach's perspective.
userStories.phase: Phase 5: Full Player Portal Remaining Sections
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/player/feedback/page.tsx
Read coachParentSummaries.ts FULLY before implementing — understand the privateInsight vs publicSummary split

Backend: add a new query getCoachFeedbackForPlayer(playerIdentityId) to coachParentSummaries.ts that returns ONLY publicSummary fields for records where status is one of: approved, auto_approved, delivered, viewed. The query MUST NOT return privateInsight or any of its nested fields.
Frontend: display results as a chronological feed, newest first
Each feedback card shows: coach name, date, AI summary text (publicSummary.text), sensitivity badge (normal=grey / injury=amber / behavior=blue)
Player can acknowledge a feedback item (calls existing acknowledge mutation, sets acknowledgedAt timestamp). Show 'Acknowledged ✓' state after.
Unacknowledged items shown with a subtle highlight or 'New' badge
The raw privateInsight text MUST NEVER appear anywhere on this page — verify this by inspecting what the query returns
All three sensitivity categories (normal, injury, behavior) are shown without restriction — this is the player's own development data
Empty state: 'Your coaches haven't shared any feedback with you yet. Feedback shared by your coach will appear here.'
Mobile-first: 375px minimum width
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: Read coachParentSummaries.ts and the existing parent portal view of coach feedback BEFORE implementing. The key insight: privateInsight is the coach's raw note (never shown), publicSummary is the AI-generated version (shown to player when status is approved/delivered/viewed). The backend query must return only publicSummary fields.

userStories.title: Phase 5 Player Portal Sections E2E Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Playwright E2E tests for the full player portal sections so that progress, sharing, injury, and coach feedback features work end-to-end.
userStories.phase: Phase 5: Full Player Portal Remaining Sections
acceptanceCriteria: Create test file: apps/web/uat/tests/player-portal-phase5.spec.ts
Test: My Progress page loads and shows sport passport ratings in read-only format
Test: My Progress — no edit buttons present on skill rating fields
Test: My Progress — player notes textarea is present and saves successfully
Test: My Passport Sharing — sharing toggle changes state and persists in DB
Test: My Injuries — page loads and shows existing injury list
Test: My Injuries — 'Report New Injury' form opens, submits, creates injury with reportedByRole 'player'
Test: My Feedback — page loads and shows approved coachParentSummaries records
Test: My Feedback — privateInsight text does NOT appear anywhere on the page
Test: My Feedback — acknowledge button sets acknowledgedAt on the record
Manual test 1: Log in as adult player with sport passport → navigate to My Progress → confirm ratings visible with sport tabs
Manual test 2: Confirm player CANNOT edit skill ratings (no edit controls present)
Manual test 3: Confirm player CAN add a personal note to passport notes section, saves successfully
Manual test 4: Navigate to My Passport Sharing → toggle sharing on → confirm sharing enabled in DB
Manual test 5: Navigate to My Injuries → confirm existing injuries shown with correct badges
Manual test 6: Click 'Report New Injury' → fill all fields → submit → confirm injury record created with reportedByRole 'player'
Manual test 7: New injury appears in list with 'You reported this' badge
Manual test 8: Log in as coach → confirm player-reported injury appears in coach injury view with 'Player-reported' badge
Manual test 9: As coach, approve a coachParentSummary for a player → log in as that player → navigate to Coach Feedback → confirm the publicSummary text appears
Manual test 10: Confirm the coach's privateInsight text does NOT appear anywhere on the feedback page
Manual test 11: Acknowledge a feedback item → confirm acknowledgedAt is set in DB and the card shows acknowledged state
Manual test 12: Confirm all three sensitivity categories (normal, injury, behavior) are visible in the player's feedback feed
userStories.notes: US-P5-001, US-P5-002, US-P5-003, and US-P5-004 must all be complete before this UAT story. Phase 1 portal layout must also be complete for navigation to work.
